I agree with the prior conclusion that optogenetics does not refer to the study of gene expression optimization. Retrieved evidence confirms that optogenetics specifically employs genetically engineered light-sensitive proteins (such as channelrhodopsins) to activate or inhibit neurons with precision, serving as a tool for controlled neural activity—not for manipulating gene expression patterns.
